title: Fenrir — Are we learning?
description: Human-readable verdict view: is Fenrir compounding (learning) or just recalling? Engineer detail lives in the 'Learning' dashboard.

options.content:
## 🧠 Fenrir — Are we actually learning?
Fenrir is **compounding** (real learning, not just recall) only if these move together over time: **Reuse ↑**, **Teacher-escalation ↓**, **Cost per solved ↓**, while **Skills ↑**. A flat **Reuse** curve is an honest *negative* result — recall, not compounding. _Benchmarks are contamination-unsafe, so absolute accuracy isn't a clean claim; the **trends** are the signal._

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS "Reuse %" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS "Escalation %" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(cost_usd) AS "Cost/solved" FROM tasks WHERE verified=true AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT created_at AS time, count(*) OVER (ORDER BY created_at) AS "Skills" FROM skills WHERE state='stable' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: ⭐ Is consolidated knowledge being REUSED?  (the headline bet)
panels.description: Share of tasks that applied a consolidated abstraction. Rising above zero = compounding demonstrated; flat zero = honest negative result (recall only).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS "Reuse %" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Is it needing the expensive teacher LESS?
panels.description: Share of tasks escalated to the paid teacher. Falling over time = the small model is internalising skills.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS "Escalation %" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Is each solved problem getting CHEAPER?
panels.description: Average $ per verified-correct task. Falling = compounding (the Nth similar task costs less).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(cost_usd) AS "Cost/solved" FROM tasks WHERE verified=true AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Skills accumulated (the durable asset)
panels.description: Cumulative crystallised, self-verified skills. The library survives model swaps.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, count(*) OVER (ORDER BY created_at) AS "Skills" FROM skills WHERE state='stable' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Memory abstractions formed by consolidation
panels.description: Cumulative generalised long-term abstractions produced by competitive replay (003-C).

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, count(*) OVER (ORDER BY created_at) AS "Abstractions" FROM long_term_memory WHERE abstraction_level>=2 AND memory_type='semantic' AND $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY 1

options.content:
## 🔬 The real test — is a skill actually *used*?
The charts above can look busy while nothing real happens: Fenrir can pile up skills it never reuses. These two cut through that. **Read them together:** first *did it even have a matching skill?* (coverage), then *when it did, did that skill let it skip the teacher?* (the bet). Everything else is noise next to these two.

panels.title: 🎯 When Fenrir HAD a matching skill — did it still need the teacher?
panels.description: The whole bet in one chart. GREEN = problems where Fenrir already had a relevant skill. GREY = problems it met cold, with nothing stored. If learning is real, the green line drops BELOW grey and keeps falling — the skill let it skip the expensive teacher. If green and grey sit together at the top, Fenrir is hoarding skills it never actually reuses: recall, not learning. This is the kill-switch. The feasibility-gated curriculum (004) is now LIVE — the per-cohort cut of this gap lives on the engineer 'Learning' board (verdict series, SC-002).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN (CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) END) AS "Had a matching skill", avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NULL THEN (CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) END) AS "Cold — no skill" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: 🧩 Did Fenrir even HAVE a relevant skill to use?
panels.description: Before reuse can pay off, a stored skill has to actually MATCH the new problem. This is how often that happened — the share of problems where a consolidated skill was retrieved at all. If this is near zero, the problem stream is too random for any skill to apply: the fix is curriculum (feed it problems near skills it already owns), not more skills. This line must rise first, before the green line above can fall. That fix — the feasibility-gated curriculum (004) — is now LIVE, biasing the task draw toward skill-adjacent problems to lift exactly this line off the ~0.8% floor; watch it climb (SC-001).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day',created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS "Had a skill ready" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
